title: Migrate root Plexus Python packaging to Poetry
description: Migrate the root Plexus Python project from setuptools/pip requirements workflow to Poetry-managed dependencies so Snyk can reliably parse and scan dependency manifests/lockfile. Scope: root project only (pyproject.toml, lockfile, docs/scripts updates). Out of scope: nested lambda/dashboard dependency trees unless explicitly requested.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Starting work. Plan: (1) inventory current root dependency and build config (pyproject + requirements), (2) convert to Poetry metadata/dependency groups, (3) generate lockfile and validate install/test commands, (4) update docs/usage for Snyk and developer workflow.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Scope update from stakeholder: remove root requirements.txt and delete the requirements-sync workflow. Poetry metadata + poetry.lock become the root dependency source of truth for Snyk.

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Implemented Poetry migration for root project scope. Changes made: switched pyproject build backend to poetry-core; replaced setuptools/project metadata with [tool.poetry] + dependency groups; added [tool.poetry.scripts]; updated tasks.py to use poetry install/run; updated README install command to poetry; updated CI and documentation workflows to install and run via Poetry; deleted .github/workflows/requirements-sync.yml; deleted root requirements.txt; generated poetry.lock. Validation: poetry check passed, poetry lock succeeded after constraining python range to >=3.11,<3.13 due numba/langchain-community solver conflict on 3.13, poetry install --with dev succeeded, poetry run pytest --version and invoke --list succeeded, and package import via poetry run python succeeded.
